title: Landing page and public web presence for loopctl.com

story.i_want_to: see a clear landing page that explains what loopctl is, how it works, and how to get started
story.so_that: I can evaluate whether loopctl fits my AI development workflow and begin onboarding

rationale: loopctl is currently a headless API with no public-facing web presence. To offer it as a service, the root domain needs a landing page that communicates the value proposition, links to documentation and API reference, and provides a path to sign up. Without this, visitors to loopctl.com hit a JSON redirect — not a viable first impression for a product.

acceptance_criteria.description: Phoenix LiveView is enabled in the endpoint (socket uncommented, :browser pipeline added to router) alongside the existing :api pipeline. API routes under /api/v1/ are unaffected.

GET / serves an HTML landing page (not a JSON redirect) with: hero section explaining loopctl in one sentence, problem/solution section, key features list (chain-of-custody, MCP integration, structural trust, multi-tenant), quick-start code snippet, and a call-to-action.

Landing page includes a navigation bar with links to: Features (anchor), Docs (link to /docs or external), API Reference (link to /swaggerui or /api/v1/openapi), GitHub repo, and a Sign Up / Get Started CTA.
Landing page includes a footer with: copyright, GitHub link, API status link (/health), and legal placeholder links (terms, privacy).
A root layout (root.html.heex) and app layout exist with proper HTML skeleton: meta tags, viewport, font loading, and Tailwind CSS v4 via the asset pipeline.
Tailwind CSS v4 is configured with a design system appropriate for a developer-tools product: dark mode first, monospace for code/IDs, clean sans-serif for body text, high contrast for readability.
The landing page is server-rendered (LiveView or dead view) — no client-side JavaScript framework required. Page loads fast with no layout shift.
The existing /health endpoint and /api/v1/* routes continue to function identically. No regression in API behavior.
The page is responsive: readable on mobile (320px), tablet (768px), and desktop (1280px+). CTA buttons have minimum 44px touch targets on mobile.
All text meets WCAG AA contrast ratios (4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for large text) against the chosen background colors.

test_cases.name: GET / returns HTML landing page with 200 status
expected_results: Body contains 'loopctl' in the title or hero

test_cases.name: API routes still return JSON and are unaffected
Landing page contains required sections
steps: GET / and parse the HTML response
expected_results: Page contains a hero section with product description
Page contains navigation with docs/API/GitHub links
Page contains a footer with copyright

test_cases.name: Static assets (CSS, JS) are served correctly
steps: GET / and extract CSS/JS asset paths from the HTML
expected_results: All referenced JS files return 200 (if any)

test_cases.name: Landing page renders on mobile viewport without horizontal scroll
steps: GET / and verify responsive meta tag is present
expected_results: HTML contains viewport meta tag with width=device-width
No fixed-width elements wider than 320px in the markup

technical_notes: Enable LiveView socket in lib/loopctl_web/endpoint.ex (uncomment the socket /live line)
Add :browser pipeline to router.ex with :accepts html, :fetch_session, :fetch_live_flash, :put_root_layout, :protect_from_forgery, :put_secure_browser_headers
Root layout: lib/loopctl_web/components/layouts/root.html.heex — HTML skeleton with meta, fonts, CSS
App layout: lib/loopctl_web/components/layouts.ex — marketing layout function component
Landing LiveView: lib/loopctl_web/live/landing_live.ex — server-rendered, no real-time features needed
Add Tailwind CSS v4 to the asset pipeline: tailwindcss CLI or esbuild plugin in config/config.exs
Design system: dark mode first, developer-tools aesthetic — think Stripe docs or Linear, not generic SaaS
Fonts: consider Inter or Geist for body, JetBrains Mono or similar for code snippets
The existing WelcomeController.redirect_to_api/2 should be replaced by the new landing route
Keep /swaggerui route available in dev/test — consider making it available in prod behind /docs/api
No authentication required for the landing page — it is fully public
Add tailwind and esbuild to mix.exs deps if not already present
